DECISION_VARIABLES.MEANING: Index set for items, 0-based indices corresponding to items 1..24 in the original data
Binary decision variable X[i] = 1 if item i is packed, 0 otherwise, for i in I

DATA_ROLES.NAME: VALUES

DATA_ROLES.NAME: CAPACITY
DATA_ROLES.MEANING: Maximum allowed total weight of packed items (kg); unit = kg

X[i] binary for i in I

FuncionDeCarga:
def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    import ast
    d = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)
    data = d.get('DATA', {})
    standardDataclass.values = data.get('POINTS', [])
    standardDataclass.weights = data.get('COSTS', [])
    standardDataclass.length = len(standardDataclass.values) if standardDataclass.values else len(standardDataclass.weights)
    standardDataclass.capacity = data.get('BUDGET', 0)
    if 'SOLUTION' in d:
        standardDataclass.solution = d['SOLUTION']
    if 'SCORE' in d:
        standardDataclass.score = d['SCORE']
    return standardDataclass

DECISION_VARIABLES.MEANING: X[i] = 1 if the offer from customer i is accepted, 0 otherwise

VOLUME.NAME: VOLUME
VOLUME.MEANING: gallons requested by each customer, aligned with index set I

CAPACITY.NAME: CAPACITY
CAPACITY.MEANING: maximum total gallons available to sell

OBJECTIVE.EXPRESSION: quicksum(PRICE[i] * X[i] for i in I)

CONSTRAINTS: quicksum(VOLUME[i] * X[i] for i in I) <= CAPACITY
X[i] in {0,1} for i in I

SOLUTION_FORMAT.DESCRIPTION: A 0-indexed, comma-separated list of customer indices to accept (e.g. "0,2,5").

def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    data = eval(extractedData)
    d = data['DATA']
    standardDataclass.length = len(d['SCORE'])

    standardDataclass.weights = list(d['TIME'])
    standardDataclass.capacity = d['BUDGET_TIME']
    standardDataclass.time = float(d['BUDGET_TIME'])
    standardDataclass.score = sum(d['SCORE'])
    return standardDataclass

def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    import ast
    d = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)
    inst = standardDataclass()
    inst.length = len(d['DATA']['ITEMS'])
    inst.values = list(d['DATA']['POINTS'])
    inst.weights = list(d['DATA']['COSTS'])
    inst.capacity = int(d['DATA']['BUDGET'])
    inst.score = 0
    inst.time = 0.0
    return inst

OBJECTIVE.EVALUATION_FUNCTION:
def eval_solution(sol):
    # sol is a list of 0-based customer indices (integers)
    # DATA.I is 1-based; convert sol (0-based) to 1-based indices for evaluation
    prices = [16,4,24,6,3,23,11,7,1,8,16,1,18,13,19,2,17,17,8,12,15,11,3,21]
    vols = [18,17,6,20,3,10,20,15,8,15,21,7,24,23,2,6,24,9,3,5,11,9,17,14]
    demand = 204
    revenue = sum(prices[i] for i in sol)
    volume = sum(vols[i] for i in sol)
    feasible = (volume >= demand)
    return {"revenue": revenue, "volume": volume, "feasible": feasible}
# Usage example (0-based indices): eval_solution([0,2,5])

CONSTRAINTS: quicksum(VOLUME[i-1]*X[i] for i in I) >= DEMAND
X[i] in {0,1} for i in I

SOLUTION_FORMAT.DESCRIPTION: 0-indexed comma-separated list of selected customer indices (e.g. "0,2,5"). Note: DATA.I is 1-based; when evaluating solutions against DATA, map each 0-based index k to DATA index (k+1) or use PRICE[k] and VOLUME[k] with 0-based lists as in the provided evaluation function.

import ast

def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    d = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)
    scores = d['DATA']['SCORE']
    times = d['DATA']['TIME']
    standardDataclass.length = len(scores)
    standardDataclass.values = list(scores)
    standardDataclass.weights = list(times)
    standardDataclass.capacity = int(d['CONSTRAINTS'][0].split('>=')[1].strip())
    return standardDataclass

SOLUTION_FORMAT.evaluation_function:
def evaluate(sol):
    # sol: list of 0-indexed item indices (0..23)
    COST=[40,20,110,20,240,50,120,240,210,200,210,230,130,210,50,80,50,80,200,90,40,150,80,60]
    POINT=[22,22,4,5,6,14,15,12,3,10,7,3,23,21,7,19,20,22,6,19,14,24,24,1]
    BUDGET=800
    total_cost = sum(COST[i] for i in sol)
    total_points = sum(POINT[i] for i in sol)
    return {'cost': total_cost, 'points': total_points, 'within_budget': total_cost <= BUDGET}
SOLUTION_FORMAT.feedback: DATA_ERROR: Potential indexing ambiguity — DATA.I is declared 1..24 while Python-style DATA lists provided are 0-indexed. Suggest explicit index mapping (either make I 0..23 or change expressions to use POINT[i-1], COST[i-1] when I is 1..24). | LOGIC_ERROR: No structural logic error found in objective or budget constraint; ensure the solver enforces binary domain X[i] in {0,1} at the model-declaration line (e.g., variable declaration line). | RESULTS_NOT_CONSISTENT: The KNOWN_RANDOM_SOLUTION [0,1,3,5,6,12,15,16,17,19,20,22] yields cost=800 and points=219 (consistent with EXPECTED_RESULT_FROM_SOLUTION). No inconsistency detected. | ACTIONABLE_SUGGESTIONS: 1) Make indexing explicit in model code to eliminate off-by-one risk. 2) Use the provided evaluation_function (ast_eval-safe) to verify candidate solutions quickly. 3) To obtain ground-truth optimality, run a 0/1 knapsack DP or a MILP solver; DP (capacity=800, n=24) will provide certifiable optimum rapidly. 4) When reporting solver code lines, reference the variable-declaration line for binary enforcement and the objective line for any operator changes.

    import ast
    data = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)

    inst.values = data['DATA']['POINT']
    inst.weights = data['DATA']['COST']
    inst.capacity = data['DATA']['BUDGET']
    return inst

DATA_ROLES.name: CAPACITY
DATA_ROLES.meaning: Maximum total gallons available

CONSTRAINTS: sum(GALLONS[i]*x[i] for i in INDEX_CUSTOMERS) <= CAPACITY
x[i] in {0,1} for i in INDEX_CUSTOMERS

SOLUTION_FORMAT.description: 0-indexed comma-separated list of customer indices to accept (each integer in 0..23)

FuncionDeCarga:
def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    import ast
    d = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)
    data = d['DATA']
    standardDataclass.length = len(data['PRICES'])
    standardDataclass.values = list(data['PRICES'])
    standardDataclass.weights = list(data['GALLONS'])
    standardDataclass.capacity = data['CAPACITY']
    return standardDataclass

I.MEANING: Index set of attractions (values 1 through N)

X.MEANING: Decision variable X[i] = 1 if attraction i is visited, 0 otherwise

SCORES.NAME: SCORES
SCORES.MEANING: Point value for each attraction in order 1..N

TIMES.NAME: TIMES
TIMES.MEANING: Time in minutes required for each attraction in order 1..N

CONSTRAINTS: index_definition: I = 1..N
time_limit: sum(TIMES[i]*X[i] for i in I) <= TIME_LIMIT
binary_domain: X[i] in {0,1} for i in I

SOLUTION_FORMAT.DESCRIPTION: Output is a comma-separated list of selected attraction indices using 0-based numbering. (Attraction 1 in DATA corresponds to 0 in the output, attraction 2 -> 1, ..., attraction 24 -> 23). Each element is an integer.

FuncionDeCarga:
import ast

def Transform(extractedData, standardDataclass):
    d = ast.literal_eval(extractedData)
    data = d['DATA']
    standardDataclass.length = data['N']
    standardDataclass.values = list(data['SCORES'])
    standardDataclass.weights = list(data['TIMES'])
    standardDataclass.capacity = data['TIME_LIMIT']
    standardDataclass.score = 0
    standardDataclass.time = 0.0
    return standardDataclass
